key_themes: AI-driven demand, record bookings, and strategic acquisitions
Q2 FY2026 earnings beat with margin expansion and raised FCF guidance
Disappointing FY2026 revenue guidance (below consensus) triggering sell-off
Attractive valuation (~15x P/E, high ROE) despite near-term growth moderation
Analyst Moderate Buy consensus with 30-60% upside to price targets
summary: Accenture posted strong Q2 FY2026 results with an EPS beat ($2.93), revenue at the high end of guidance, record $22.1B bookings (AI-related strength), and raised full-year FCF outlook, while continuing to gain market share. However, the April FY2026 revenue guidance ($71.8-73.2B) missed consensus (~$73.9B), contributing to a sharp sell-off that took the stock near 52-week lows around $184-198 amid broader IT sector weakness and federal spending concerns. Despite the near-term caution, robust AI momentum, acquisitions, profitability, and compelling valuation are driving positive long-term investor and analyst sentiment, with a Moderate Buy rating and substantial price target upside.[[1]](https://www.marketbeat.com/instant-alerts/accenture-nyseacn-releases-fy-2026-earnings-guidance-2026-04-09/)[[2]](https://newsroom.accenture.com/news/2026/accenture-reports-second-quarter-fiscal-2026-results)[[1]](https://www.marketbeat.com/instant-alerts/accenture-nyseacn-releases-fy-2026-earnings-guidance-2026-04-09/)
sources: 10+ web news/articles on Q2 FY26 earnings (Mar 2026), April FY2026 guidance update, and analyst ratings, 20+ recent X posts from semantic search and keyword searches for $ACN (Jan-Apr 2026), Yahoo Finance, MarketBeat, Seeking Alpha, company press releases, and investor commentary
